problem.text: An even number of circles are nested, starting with a radius of $1$ and increasing by $1$ each time, all sharing a common point. The region between every other circle is shaded, starting with the region inside the circle of radius $2$ but outside the circle of radius $1$. What is the **least number of circles** needed to make the total shaded area at least $2023\pi$?
problem.highlight: Find the least number of circles for at least $2023\pi$ shaded area.

viz_requirements.description: Coordinate plane with nested circles and shaded areas.

actions.description: Draw nested circles with increasing radii.
Reveal area formulas for each circle.
Highlight the shaded area between circles.
Animate a graph showing the relationship between n and shaded area.
Emphasize the inequality for determining minimum circles.

nodes.title: Introduction to Nested Circles
nodes.objective: Introduce the concept of nested circles.
nodes.context_from_previous: Students are familiar with basic geometry and area formulas.

beat_outline.narration_hint: Let’s visualize circles nested together, each increasing by one radius.

nodes.title: Area of Circles
nodes.objective: Explain how to calculate the area of a circle.
nodes.context_from_previous: Students have seen the introduction to nested circles.

beat_outline.narration_hint: The area of a circle is calculated using A = πr².

nodes.title: Shaded Area Calculation
nodes.objective: Derive the shaded area between circles.
nodes.context_from_previous: Students understand how to calculate the area of a circle.

beat_outline.narration_hint: The shaded area is the difference between the areas of two circles.

nodes.title: Summing Up the Shaded Areas
nodes.objective: Introduce the concept of summing shaded areas.
nodes.context_from_previous: Students have learned how to calculate individual shaded areas.

beat_outline.narration_hint: Now, let's sum the areas of all shaded regions.

nodes.title: Final Area Formula
nodes.objective: Present the total shaded area formula.
nodes.context_from_previous: Students understand the concept of summing shaded areas.

beat_outline.narration_hint: We can now express the total shaded area as a formula.

nodes.title: Finding the Minimum Circles
nodes.objective: Solve for the minimum number of circles needed for the shaded area.
nodes.context_from_previous: Students have learned the formula for the total shaded area.

beat_outline.narration_hint: Let’s determine how many circles we need to reach at least 2023π in shaded area.

nodes.question_hint: What is the area of a circle with radius 3?
nodes.wrong_path_hint: Remember, area is calculated using the radius squared.

wrong_path_acts.title: Area of Circles
wrong_path_acts.objective: Explain how to calculate the area of a circle.
wrong_path_acts.context_from_previous: Students have seen the introduction to nested circles.

beat_outline.narration_hint: The area of a circle is calculated using A = πr².

nodes.question_hint: What is the shaded area between circles of radius 2 and 1?
nodes.wrong_path_hint: Ensure you are calculating the difference in areas.

wrong_path_acts.title: Shaded Area Calculation
wrong_path_acts.objective: Derive the shaded area between circles.
wrong_path_acts.context_from_previous: Students understand how to calculate the area of a circle.

beat_outline.narration_hint: The shaded area is the difference between the areas of two circles.

nodes.question_hint: How many circles are needed to achieve a shaded area of at least 2023π?
nodes.wrong_path_hint: Consider how the shaded area grows with more circles.

wrong_path_acts.title: Final Area Formula
wrong_path_acts.objective: Present the total shaded area formula.
wrong_path_acts.context_from_previous: Students understand the concept of summing shaded areas.

beat_outline.narration_hint: We can now express the total shaded area as a formula.
